info.title: VerifyYou API
info.description: The VerifyYou developer API. Start a verification from the browser with your publishable key, then confirm the result server-side with your secret key. Authenticate every request with `Authorization: Bearer <key>`.

post.description:
Initialize a session before the user verifies and get back a hosted verification link with a queued-up session. Redirect the user to the returned `url`; they come back to the redirect URL configured on the verification with `?vyt=<token>&vyc=<0|1>`, which you confirm server-side at `GET /v3/confirmations/{token}`.

Accepts a **publishable key** (browser) or a **secret key** (server). `email`, `phone`, `external_id`, `verification_id`, and `verification_external_id` require a secret key. See each parameter below for what it does and when it's required.

get.description: Exchange the `vyt` token the user returned with for the authoritative verification result. **Secret key only.** Call this from your backend before granting access.

parameters.name: token

schema.description: The `vyt` confirmation token the user returned with after verifying.
schema.title: Token

parameters.description: The `vyt` confirmation token the user returned with after verifying.

post.description: Mark a confirmation as used so the same `vyt` token can't be confirmed again: single-use enforcement. **Secret key only.** After locking, `GET /v3/confirmations/{token}` returns `verified: false`.

parameters.name: token

schema.description: The `vyt` confirmation token the user returned with after verifying.
schema.title: Token

parameters.description: The `vyt` confirmation token the user returned with after verifying.

post.description: Check that an API key works and see what it is: publishable or secret, test or live, and which company it belongs to. Send the key as the bearer token, like any other call. Always returns `200`: a bad key comes back as `valid: false` rather than an error, so it's safe to wire into a healthcheck or setup step.

phone.title: Phone
phone.description: The phone the flow collected/verified, if any.

email.title: Email
email.description: The email the flow collected/verified, if any.

verified.title: Verified
verified.description: Authoritative result: true only if the verification passed and the confirmation is unlocked. Gate access on this, not on the client-side `vyc` hint.

id.title: Id
id.description: The confirmation id for this person's pass through the verification.

status.title: Status
status.description: Verdict detail: pending, approved, flagged (passed with findings), denied.

reasons.title: Reasons
reasons.description: Rule findings behind the status (e.g. collision, identity_mismatch).

confirmed_at.title: Confirmed At
confirmed_at.description: When the latest settled verdict landed.

identity.description: The identity collected during the flow, present only when the verification's identity config has sharing enabled. Secret-key responses only.

external_id.title: External Id
external_id.description: The `external_id` attached to the session at initialize, if any.

verification_id.title: Verification Id
verification_id.description: Secret key only. Target a verification directly instead of running the company's default verification.

verification_external_id.title: Verification External Id
verification_external_id.description: Secret key only. Target a verification by your own external id for it (set when the verification was created), as an alternative to `verification_id`.

email.title: Email
email.description: Secret key only. Attach a stable identity to the verification. Fails if that identity is already in use.

phone.title: Phone
phone.description: Secret key only. Attach a stable identity to the verification. Fails if that identity is already in use.

external_id.title: External Id
external_id.description: Secret key only. Link this verification to your own user or record id.

external_tracker.title: External Tracker
external_tracker.description: Non-PII label echoed back in the flow webhook payload. Use it to correlate funnel events with your own analytics.

pass_params.title: Pass Params
pass_params.description: Extra query params to carry through the flow and append to the return URL. Reserved `vy*` keys are ignored.

url.title: Url
url.description: Hosted verification link with a queued-up session. Redirect the user here.

valid.title: Valid
valid.description: Whether the key resolved to an active account. An unknown or revoked key comes back as `false`, not an error.

key_type.title: Key Type
key_type.description: Prefix of the key you sent: `pk_live`, `pk_test`, `sk_live`, `sk_test`, or `unknown` if it matched none of them.

is_test.title: Is Test
is_test.description: Whether the key is a test key. `null` when the key didn't resolve.

customer_id.title: Customer Id
customer_id.description: The company the key belongs to. `null` when the key didn't resolve.

locked.title: Locked
locked.description: True once the confirmation is locked. A locked confirmation can't be confirmed again; `GET /v3/confirmations/{token}` then returns `verified: false`.

external_id.title: External Id
external_id.description: Your `external_id` for the verification, if one was set at initialize.

external_tenant_id.title: External Tenant Id
external_tenant_id.description: Your `external_tenant_id` for the verification, if one was set.

tags.name: Verification Flow
tags.description: Start a hosted verification and get the URL to redirect the user to.

tags.name: Confirmations
tags.description: Confirm a verification token, and lock it against replay (secret key).

tags.name: API Keys
tags.description: Check whether a publishable or secret key is valid.
